data.title: Ethereum News: $105M Ether Shorts Liquidated as Eric Trump Warns Bears “Stop Betting Against BTC and ETH”
data.description: Key TakeawaysEther surged past $4,000 for the first time in eight months, triggering $105 million in short liquidations — the largest among all crypto assets on Friday.Eric Trump told his 5.8M X followers to “stop betting” against Bitcoin and Ether, calling out bearish traders.Analysts say $4,100 is the key level that could trigger a rapid short squeeze to $4,400–$4,500.Spot Ether ETFs have attracted $537 million in inflows over the past four days, adding to bullish momentum.Price targets from traders and analysts range from $10,000 to $16,000.ETH Price Surge Triggers Largest Short Wipeout of the DayEther (ETH) jumped above $4,000 on Friday for the first time since December 2024, forcing a wave of liquidations in the derivatives market. Data from CoinGlass shows $105 million in Ether short positions were liquidated in 24 hours — accounting for 53% of the $199.6 million total shorts liquidated across the crypto market.The rally pushed ETH as high as $4,060 before easing to $4,015 at publication time, a 4.6% daily gain, according to Nansen.Eric Trump Taunts BearsThe rally drew attention from Eric Trump, son of U.S. President Donald Trump, who posted on X:“It puts a smile on my face to see ETH shorts get smoked today. Stop betting against BTC and ETH — you will be run over.”His comments come as both Bitcoin and Ether continue to attract institutional inflows, with traders speculating that momentum could accelerate if ETH breaks key resistance.$4,100 Seen as Breakout Level for Short SqueezeCrypto trader Ash Crypto said the $4,100 level is “major resistance,” and a breakout could trigger a short squeeze to $4,400–$4,500 within hours.Market sentiment has been bolstered by strong demand for spot Ether ETFs, which saw $537 million in inflows over the past four trading days, according to Farside data.Trader Ted said, “It’s never been this bullish in my opinion,” citing increasing institutional interest and ETF-driven demand.Bullish Long-Term PredictionsFollowing ETH’s breakout, crypto trader Moustache predicted the asset could reach “$10,000+” in the next cycle.Meanwhile, Tom Lee, co-founder of Fundstrat, compared Ether’s current trajectory to Bitcoin’s 2017 bull run, saying ETH could climb to $16,000.

data.title: Bitcoin News: Harvard Endowment Reveals $116M Stake in BlackRock Bitcoin ETF
data.description: Key TakeawaysHarvard Management Company disclosed 1.9M shares in BlackRock’s iShares Bitcoin ETF, worth $116M as of June 30.The Bitcoin ETF stake ranks as Harvard’s fifth-largest holding, behind Microsoft, Amazon, Booking Holdings, and Meta.Harvard’s $53.2B endowment is the largest among U.S. universities, historically focused on tech-heavy allocations.The BlackRock Bitcoin ETF — approved by the SEC in January 2024 — has grown to $86B in net assets.SEC’s new options contract limit increase could boost ETF demand further.Largest U.S. University Endowment Adds Significant Bitcoin ExposureHarvard Management Company, which oversees the $53.2 billion Harvard University endowment, has disclosed a substantial position in BlackRock’s iShares Bitcoin ETF (IBIT), signaling growing institutional comfort with crypto exposure.According to a Friday SEC filing, Harvard held roughly 1.9 million IBIT shares valued at more than $116 million as of June 30, 2025. This makes the Bitcoin ETF Harvard’s fifth-largest reported holding for the quarter, after Microsoft, Amazon, Booking Holdings, and Meta.Institutional Adoption Gains MomentumWhile Harvard has been primarily tech-focused in 2025, reports suggest it explored crypto investments as early as 2018. Its disclosure follows other universities, such as Emory University, which in 2024 reported buying 2.7M shares of the Grayscale Bitcoin Mini Trust, then worth $15M.The move reflects a broader trend of U.S. institutional investors — including major endowments — integrating digital assets into diversified portfolios.From SEC Approval to University PortfoliosThe SEC approved 11 spot Bitcoin ETFs, including BlackRock’s IBIT, in January 2024. Since launch, IBIT has grown to $86 billion in net assets, making it one of the largest Bitcoin investment vehicles.In a recent policy shift, the SEC announced it will raise the options contract limit for all ETFs — including IBIT — from 25,000 to 250,000 contracts, a change analysts say could boost trading activity and investor demand.

data.title: Trader Eugene Ng Ah Sio Shifts Strategy After $4,000 Resistance Break
data.description: According to BlockBeats, trader Eugene Ng Ah Sio announced a change in his trading strategy following the breach of the $4,000 resistance level. Ng Ah Sio expressed his decision to switch to a bullish stance, stating, "There's nothing more to say, the $4,000 resistance has been broken, so I surrender and go long." He now anticipates guidance from Tom Lee, the newly appointed chairman of BitMine and a prominent advocate for Ethereum, to lead the way forward.

data.title: Ethereum's Leverage Risks Highlighted Amid Growing Investor Interest
data.description: According to Foresight News, Ethereum co-founder Vitalik Buterin recently expressed concerns about the potential risks of excessive leverage in the market, despite the expansion of channels for investors to acquire ETH by the Ethereum Treasury Company. Vitalik warned that improper handling could lead to a 'game of excessive leverage.'However, the market appears to be largely ignoring Vitalik's warnings. The holdings of three major ETH micro-strategies have surpassed those of the Ethereum Foundation, with many investors focusing on figures like Tom Lee instead.Vitalik's concerns are primarily centered on the risks associated with leverage. In contrast, the Ethereum Foundation continues to sell ETH, which could pose a greater risk to the market. Companies such as SBET and BitMine, listed on the U.S. stock market, are accumulating ETH through leverage. It seems that market participants are more interested in increasing their ETH holdings to achieve returns rather than worrying about the potential risks of leverage.

data.title: U.S. Tariff Rates Reach Historic Highs, WTO and IMF Report
data.description: According to PANews, recent data from a tariff tracking tool developed by the World Trade Organization and the International Monetary Fund reveals a significant increase in the United States' trade-weighted average tariff rate. As of August 7, 2025, the rate has surged to 20.11%, a substantial rise from 2.44% at the beginning of the year. The simple average tariff rate on all U.S. global goods has also increased from 2.08% to 17.39% over the same period.The impact of these tariff changes is evident in the global trade volume affected, which has skyrocketed from $288.46 billion at the start of the year to $2.747 trillion currently. A report released on August 7 by Yale University's Budget Laboratory, which has been monitoring the effects of U.S. tariff policies over time, indicates that the average effective tariff rate has now reached 18.6%. This marks the highest level since 1933, reflecting the significant adjustments in U.S. tariff policies.

data.title: Hong Kong Insurance Sector Embraces AI and Blockchain for Transformation
data.description: According to PANews, Hong Kong's Secretary for Financial Services and the Treasury, Christopher Hui, announced on social media that local insurance companies are set to integrate advanced technologies such as AI, big data, and blockchain to drive industry transformation. AXA, a leading insurance company, has already relocated its registration back to Hong Kong as part of this initiative.

data.title: Whales Acquire 50 Million XRP in Recent Transactions
data.description: According to PANews, blockchain analyst Ali (@ali_charts) revealed on the X platform that whales have purchased approximately 50 million XRP over the past 48 hours.

data.title: Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang Tops Fortune's 2025 Influential Business Leaders List
data.description: According to PANews, on August 5, Fortune magazine released its 2025 list of the world's 100 most influential business figures. Nvidia's founder and CEO, Jensen Huang, claimed the top spot for the first time, surpassing last year's leader, Tesla founder Elon Musk. Microsoft CEO Satya Nadella, Meta founder Mark Zuckerberg, and Musk ranked second to fourth, respectively. Google CEO Sundar Pichai, OpenAI founder Sam Altman, JPMorgan Chase CEO Jamie Dimon, and General Motors CEO Mary Barra were placed sixth, eighth, ninth, and tenth, respectively.

data.title: Circle Reports Increase in USDC Circulation Amid Recent Issuance and Redemption Activity
data.description: According to PANews, official data reveals that Circle issued approximately 49 billion USDC and redeemed around 42 billion USDC over the seven days leading up to August 7, 2025. This activity resulted in an increase of about 8 billion USDC in circulation. The total circulation of USDC now stands at 64.6 billion, supported by reserves totaling approximately $64.7 billion. These reserves include around $9 billion in cash and $55.8 billion held in the Circle Reserve Fund.

data.title: XRP price prediction: This indicator hints at 640% rally
data.description:
With XRP flying high as investors react to the resolution of the Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) case, on-chain data suggests that more upside for the asset might be on the horizon. This projected rally is rooted in the fact that XRP’s Market Value to Realized Value (MVRV) ratio has formed a golden cross, a technical pattern that occurs when the short-term MVRV ratio moves above its long-term 200-day moving average (MA). XRP MVRV chart. Source: Santiment This rare event has occurred only twice in recent years, and both times it preceded major rallies, as observed by cryptocurrency trading analyst Ali Martinez in an X post on August 8
source: https://finbold.com/xrp-price-prediction-this-indicator-hints-at-640-rally/
